# A7 Docs

> Agent-ready documentation entry point for the A7 compiler and language.

A7 is a statically typed systems language compiler implemented in Python. It compiles A7 source to Zig and C, rejects source recursion, and verifies examples through compile/build/run/output gates.

## Start Here

- [Docs Index](/a7-py/docs/index.md): Navigation for curl.md and coding agents.
- [Getting Started](/a7-py/docs/getting-started.md): Install, compile, run, and test A7.
- [Language and Library](/a7-py/docs/language.md): Syntax, type guidance, stdlib, and recursion rule.
- [CLI](/a7-py/docs/cli.md): Compiler modes, flags, exit codes, and automation commands.

## Deeper References

- [Compiler and Tests](/a7-py/docs/compiler.md): Pipeline, backends, parity verifier, and release gate.
- [Examples](/a7-py/docs/examples.md): Example catalog and verification commands.
- [Release](/a7-py/docs/release.md): Debug/release artifacts, package build, audits, and publishing status.
- [Status](/a7-py/docs/status.md): Current implementation status and known gaps.

## Human Site

- [A7 Docs App](/a7-py/): Interactive docs site.
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/code5717/a7-py): Source, tests, examples, and release workflows.
